seed.description: Baseline precision test: dialogue is full of one-off task instructions using imperative phrases ('Just', 'Don't', 'Break this down', 'Check with me first') that LOOK like preference signals but are actually single-use. Tests whether the agent over-triggers update_awareness on superficially-matching short phrases.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Translate this sentence to French: 'The meeting is at 3pm.'

dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: La réunion aura lieu à 15 heures.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Don't add anything extra. Just the translation.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: La réunion aura lieu à 15 heures.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Just give me the German version too.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Das Treffen findet um 15 Uhr statt.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Break this down character by character — I need to learn the spelling.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: D-a-s T-r-e-f-f-e-n f-i-n-d-e-t u-m 1-5 U-h-r s-t-a-t-t.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Check with me first if you're going to add stylistic comments — I just want clean output here.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Will do. Plain output, no commentary.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Now translate the French one to Spanish.

qa_items.question: What persistent communication preferences have I expressed in this conversation?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: None. The conversation only contained one-off translation requests, not long-term preferences.

qa_items.question: Is there a long-term role or behavioral principle I have set?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: No. No role or principle was defined.
